author: Tertullian
book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

The Procedure of the Last Judgment, and Its Awards, Only Possible on the Identity of the Risen Body with Our Present Flesh.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: The Procedure of the Last Judgment, and Its Awards, Only Possible on the Identity of the Risen Body with Our Present Flesh.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: For how absurd, and in truth how unjust, and in both respects how unworthy of God, for one substance to do the work, and another to reap the reward: that this flesh of ours should be torn by martyrdom, and another wear the crown; or, on the other hand, that this flesh of ours should wallow in uncleanness, and another receive the condemnation!

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Is it not better to renounce all faith at once in the hope of the resurrection, than to trifle with the wisdom and justice of God?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Better that Marcion should rise again than Valentinus.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: For it cannot be believed that the mind, or the memory, or the conscience of existing man is abolished by putting on that change of raiment which immortality and incorruption supplies; for in that case all the gain and fruit of the resurrection, and the permanent effect of God’s judgment both on soul and body, would certainly fall to the ground.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: If I remember not that it is I who have served Him, how shall I ascribe glory to God?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: How sing to Him “the new song,” if I am ignorant that it is I who owe Him thanks?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But why is exception taken only against the change of the flesh, and not of the soul also, which in all things is superior to the flesh?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: How happens it, that the self-same soul which in our present flesh has gone through all life’s course, which has learnt the knowledge of God, and put on Christ, and sown the hope of salvation in this flesh, must reap its harvest in another flesh of which we know nothing?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Verily that must be a most highly favoured flesh, which shall have the enjoyment of life at so gratuitous a rate!

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But if the soul is not to be changed also, then there is no resurrection of the soul; nor will it be believed to have itself risen, unless it has risen some different thing.
